meta.what: A SIMULATED, paired SNHP negotiation at a boba shop: your agent trades on the things you don't care about (when you pick up, which topping, how many) for a better price — never above the menu.
meta.price_guarantee: You never pay above the menu — a discount-only rule enforced in the code.
meta.margin_note: The shop discounts only against value the deal creates: waste it clears and batch savings bank immediately, while freed-capacity value is realized only when the freed slot refills. Net figures are simulated across a season, which models slot refill and cannibalization.
meta.attestation: Attestation is proof from your ordering app of the commitments the deal is priced on — the pickup slot you actually take, the order you actually place — so a discount is earned by what you did, not what you claimed. It never asks your budget; it's what stops customers gaming the quote.
meta.take_rate: Pricing TBD. The figures shown are gross of any SNHP fee.
meta.basis: Simulated, not a real shop's books: paired Monte-Carlo — each dynamic day run against the same crowd as a strong, profit-optimal modeled menu (not a strawman), 30 paired days.

headline.note: Simulated vs a strong, profit-optimal modeled menu (not a strawman), 30 paired days. Worst case (everyone lies, no attestation) = exactly the menu.

timing.shop: moved the order past the lunch peak to when the afternoon barista is on — frees a peak slot IF a hurry-customer refills it

group.shop: one prep run for the whole order — batch savings the shop banks immediately

now.lever: Pearls from the batch about to expire
now.shop: cleared tapioca it would have tossed at close — waste, banked immediately
